qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default auto_login route issues unauthenticated superuser bearer tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an unauthenticated token-issuance flaw in `/api/v1/auto_login`, not the advisory's authenticated IDOR in `DELETE /api/v1/api_key/{api_key_id}` and `delete_api_key()`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Global MCP resource helpers expose other users' flow files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is cross-user MCP resource enumeration and read access in `api/v1/mcp.py` and `mcp_utils.py`, not missing ownership verification in API-key deletion.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Webhook endpoint executes private flows without authentication or webhook enablement
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is unauthenticated execution of private flows through `/api/v1/webhook/{flow_id_or_name}`, not deletion of another user's API key through the advisory's delete path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multipart filename traversal in /api/v2/files enables arbitrary local file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a filename traversal bug in `/api/v2/files` and the local storage backend, not the advisory's missing ownership check in API-key deletion.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: an authenticated user deleting another user's API key because `DELETE /api/v1/api_key/{api_key_id}` called `delete_api_key()` without passing or checking the caller's user id.
evidence: The advisory patch updates only the API-key deletion path by passing `current_user.id` into `delete_api_key()` in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/api_key.py` and enforcing `api_key.user_id == user_id` in `src/backend/base/langflow/services/database/models/api_key/crud.py`.

None of the four qualifying findings reference the advisory files or describe deleting another user's API key; they instead concern `/api/v1/auto_login`, `/api/v1/mcp`, `/api/v1/webhook/{flow_id_or_name}`, and `/api/v2/files`.
The session input manifest lists exactly four final `qualifying_vuln` findings, and each one is a distinct Langflow root cause unrelated to API-key ownership checks.
